rulings.Case Title: RICHARD HEVENER et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint For Indemnification And Contribution Against Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 26, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Sacramento Stucco Company, Inc.¿s motion for leave to file cross-complaint for indemnification and contribution against Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc. is granted. No opposition filed. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD HEVENER et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 26, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Alta Building Material¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar. Defendant was dismissed on October 22. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JACK STURGES VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 26, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Neles-Jamesbury, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing packing from a Jamesbury valve attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Ay¿s declaration are sustained. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH THORNTON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 26, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Sepco Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Mr. Bradshaw¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether plaintiff worked with a gasket attributable to defendant. The objections to Mr. Bradshaw¿s declaration are overruled. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT CHARTIER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Appoint Paula Chartier As Successor-In-Interest To, And Substitute Successor-In-Interest For, Deceased Plaintiff And For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 26, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Plaintiff¿s motion to appoint Paula Chartier as successor-in-interest to, and substitute successor-in-interest for Robert Chartier, deceased plaintiff and for leave to file first amended complaint is granted. No opposition filed. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GIOVANNI COLOMBO VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 26, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant West Marine Products, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the loss of consortium claim, denied as to the negligence and strict liability claims and taken off calendar as to the false representation claim and request for punitive damages. Plaintiff¿s deposition, plaintiff¿s declaration and the declaration of Mr. Ay create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to an asbestos-containing gasket attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s deposition, plaintiff¿s declaration and the declaration of Mr. Ay are overruled. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production on the issue of whether plaintiff has an asbestos-related illness. Although the complaint appears to allege a loss of consortium claim, there is no basis for such a claim since Mr. Colombo is the sole plaintiff. Plaintiff has dismissed his false representation claim and request for punitive damages against defendant. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH RILEY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 26, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Defendant Underground Construction Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff did not present evidence creating a triable issue that he was exposed to an asbestos-containing cement pipe attributable that defendant worked with. Defendant¿s foundation objection to Mr. Ay¿s declaration is sustained. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN CASEY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 26, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 8. Defendant Dynalectric Company¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. The declarations of Mr. Gheno and Kenneth Cohen create a triable issue whether Mr. Casey was exposed to asbestos-containing fireproofing attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objection to Mr. Gheno¿s declaration and objection 2 to Kenneth Cohen¿s declaration are overruled. Defendant failed to satisfy its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and are not likely to be able to obtain evidence that defendant acted negligently. The evidentiary materials submitted with the reply papers were not considered. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JUDY CRAWFORD VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 26, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 9. Defendant Supro Corporation¿s motion to dismiss is granted. No opposition filed. Hearing required unless defendant informs Ms. Crawford of the tentative ruling and she states that she does not wish to contest. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH MCGREW VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, October 26, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 10. Defendant AT&T Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs did not present admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. McGrew was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Arts¿ declaration are sustained. = (220/HEK)
